news.summary: US President Donald Trump on Monday suggested the war with Iran could end soon but gave no clear timeline for halting attacks that have rattled the Middle East and global markets. Stocks rose after he told CBS the US-Israeli assault was “very complete,” though later remarks offered mixed signals.

news.headline: TikTok gets green light to stay in Canada, reversing earlier ban
news.summary:
Canada will allow TikTok to continue operating in the country, a complete reversal after the government had previously ordered the social media company to close its Canadian division for security reasons.

news.headline: G7 to take 'necessary measures' to support energy supplies
news.summary: Finance ministers and the IEA discussed options for stabilising oil prices which surpassed $100.

news.headline: China investors energise Hong Kong biotech stocks. Is foreign money missing out?
news.summary:
Mainland Chinese investors can now buy shares in more than a dozen newly added Hong Kong-listed biotech and pharmaceutical companies after a reshuffle of the Stock Connect southbound trading list took effect on Monday, reflecting the sector’s growing role on the international stage.

news.headline: HRW accuses Israel of using white phosphorus over Lebanese town
news.summary: Human Rights Watch on Monday accused Israel of unlawfully using white phosphorus munitions over residential areas of the southern Lebanese town of Yohmor on March 3. news.headline: Where do the 35 million foreigners living in the GCC come from?

news.headline: US blacklists Sudanese Muslim Brotherhood as ‘terrorist’ group
news.summary: Trump administration accuses the group of receiving support from Iran and carrying out violence against civilians.

news.headline: Australia urged to protect Iran women's football team as they prepare to fly home
news.summary: Concern has grown for team after one critic called them "wartime traitors" for failing to salute during the Iranian anthem.
